finding: `TASK-TEAM-0076` added a seven-layer CID trace and per-surface queue projection in Broker/Team planning, but `next --claim` does not consume queue state. A waiting task is still rejected by broad shared backlog/map and CID overlap instead of receiving a per-file queued admission.
expectedBehavior: Every task claim must run the same seven-layer CID decision, use queue state for confirmed shared file/surface conflicts, and return a bounded wait/next-file admission rather than a global overlap deadlock.
evidenceOrRepro: Post-0076 dogfood: `TASK-AAO-0158` remains blocked by `next --claim` on shared backlog/map and CID overlap although 0076 is closed and the queue should permit file-ordered progress.
followUp: Create follow-up for `next.ts` admission integration, queue-aware claim responses, stale-owner release, and a two-task dogfood regression. Keep 0076's trace/queue modules as the shared source of truth.
